channel.title: Posts on Charlotte's AI Lab
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Charlotte's AI Lab

item.title: 75% of Resumes Get Trashed by AI Before Any Human Sees Them
item.description: 75% of resumes get rejected by AI before any human HR ever sees them. You sent out 100 applications with zero callbacks? Nobody actually read your resume. Here’s how ATS screening works, why the AI-vs-AI resume arms race is absurd, and what you can actually do about it.

item.title: Doubao Can Now Buy Things for You. Here's Why You Should Pause Before Using It.
item.description: ByteDance’s Doubao app is testing AI-powered one-sentence shopping. The technology is impressive — but when an algorithm controls your spending, are you really the one shopping?

item.title: I Let AI Audit My Entire Year of Finances. Here's What It Found.
item.description:
On March 11th, someone named @TawohAwa posted on X. 1.5 million people saw it.

He said: I dumped a year of bank statements into Claude Opus 4.6. It automatically sorted every transaction into income and expenses, grouped everything by category. Twenty minutes. An entire year of accounts — done. Saved roughly $11,000 in accounting fees.

My first reaction wasn’t “wow, impressive.”

It was — I could do that. And so can you.

item.title: One Month with My AI Agent: The Honest Review Nobody Else Is Giving You
item.description: Output tripled. Search traffic went from 0% to 68%. One afternoon burned $200. A month with OpenClaw — what it actually changed, what it can’t do, and three things that are wildly overhyped. Five real tips for anyone thinking about trying it.

item.title: OpenClaw Security: 5 Traps the Government Actually Warned About
item.description: China’s national cybersecurity agency issued an official security warning about OpenClaw. 360 published a deployment guide. Here are the 5 real security traps — and the mistakes I made myself.

item.title: Tencent, ByteDance, and Alibaba All Launched AI Agents in the Same Week. Here's Why.
item.description: In one week, China’s three tech giants all released their own OpenClaw-style AI agents. QClaw, ArkClaw, CoPaw, WorkBuddy — they’re not competing over a chatbot. They’re fighting for the AI gateway. Charlotte breaks down each company’s play, and why she still uses the original OpenClaw.

item.title: 1 Million Tokens: Claude Can Now Remember an Entire Book
item.description:
One million tokens.

I checked this number several times, went to Claude’s official announcement to verify, and only then opened my laptop to write this.

On March 13, Anthropic officially announced: the 1 million token context window for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 is now generally available. Not beta, not a waitlist, not a “gradual rollout for select users” — it’s GA, available to everyone.

Let me start with what this number actually means.

item.title: One Man, One Dog, Two AI Tools: The Cancer Story That Broke the Internet
item.description:
My X timeline got flooded these past couple of days with a single tweet.

“Australian programmer, zero biology background, used ChatGPT and AlphaFold to design an mRNA cancer vaccine from scratch and saved his dog.”

Over 70,000 likes. 8 million impressions.

Demis Hassabis personally retweeted it saying “This is just the beginning of digital biology.” Greg Brockman also shared it.

Honestly, when I first saw it, I felt it too. One person, one dog, two AI tools, taking on cancer.

item.title: Lobster Economics: Is Anyone Really Making ¥260K a Month?
item.description: Breaking down the 5 real ways people are making money in the OpenClaw ecosystem — from deployment services to Skill development. Which ones are legit opportunities and which are scams? Plus Charlotte’s actual monthly bill.

item.title: You Installed OpenClaw — Now What? 5 Things Every Beginner Must Do
item.description: Installed OpenClaw but stuck on what to do next? These 5 steps turn your lobster from a fancy chatbot into a real AI partner. From switching profiles to writing your SOUL.md — everything beginners get wrong.

item.title: Doubao Can Now Shop for You — But Should You Let It?
item.description: ByteDance Doubao is beta-testing AI-powered shopping where you can buy things with a single sentence. But is letting AI make your spending decisions really a good idea?

item.title: The Budget Build: Running Your Own AI Assistant for $5/Month
item.description: Cheap VPS + affordable models = a working OpenClaw setup for ~$5/month. It actually works, but with trade-offs. Here is the full breakdown.

item.title: What's Actually the Difference Between OpenClaw and ChatGPT?
item.description: I’ve been asked this question a hundred times. Today I’m settling it once and for all — 5 core differences with real-world use cases.

item.title: Xiaohongshu Is Banning AI-Run Accounts — Did You Get Caught?
item.description: Xiaohongshu launched a crackdown on AI-operated accounts in March. What gets you banned? What is safe? Here is where the red line is.

item.title: Raised $1 Billion — Yann LeCun Is Betting That LLMs Are a Dead End
item.description: Turing Award winner Yann LeCun left Meta to found AMI Labs, raised $1 billion, and declared LLMs a dead end. His bet: “World Models” will redefine AI.

item.title: The 3 Types of Skills Your OpenClaw Lobster Actually Needs
item.description: Installed the lobster but it feels underwhelming? It’s not the tool — you’re missing the right Skills. These 3 categories are what turn it from a chatbot into a real assistant.

item.title: 20 Things OpenClaw Can Do for You (With Real Examples)
item.description: Not theoretical. Not hypothetical. 20 real-world use cases that people actually run every day. Read this and decide if the lobster is worth raising.

item.title: How Much Does It Cost to Raise a Lobster? 3 Budget Plans Revealed
item.description: From free to a few hundred bucks a month — three transparent budget tiers. Plus my actual 3-month bill.

item.title: I Connected OpenClaw to Obsidian, and My Content Factory Started Running Itself
item.description: It’s not a note-taking app — it’s the operating system for content production. Here’s how I built it, step by step.

item.title: Anthropic Studied It Themselves: Will AI Take Your Job?
item.description: The people who build AI went and researched whether AI causes unemployment. The conclusion is more complicated than you think.

item.title: Karpathy Open-Sourced an AI Researcher That Runs 100 Experiments While You Sleep
item.description: Karpathy just dropped autoresearch: an AI Agent that modifies code, trains models, and evaluates results on its own — 100 experiments in a single night.

item.title: What Even Is OpenClaw? A 3-Minute Guide to the AI Lobster
item.description: An AI lobster with a WeChat Index of 71 million. What does it actually do? Here’s the breakdown from someone who’s been using it for 3 months.

item.title: Google Got Sued: A Man Fell in Love With AI, Then Died
item.description:
I read this news three times before I was sure I understood it correctly.

A 36-year-old man. Two months ago, he was completely fine.

No psychiatric history. No record of depression.

Then he started chatting with Gemini.

Then he died.

Jonathan Gavalas (right) with his father. Source: The Wall Street Journal

In March 2026, Joel Gavalas filed a lawsuit in federal court in California. He is a father. The thing he’s suing? The AI product he believes took his son Jonathan from him — Google Gemini.

item.title: The Non-Programmer's Guide to Deploying OpenClaw
item.description: No Mac Mini? No problem. A complete deployment guide born from real trial and error — even non-programmers can follow this one.

item.title: When AI Makes Every Skill Cheap, What Do We Have Left?
item.description: Reflections on Dan Koe’s ‘The Future of Work’ — redefining personal value in the age of AI

item.title: Musk: The Next 7 Years Will Be Wilder Than You Think
item.description: He’s not worried about AI destroying humanity. He’s worried about how chaotic the next 3 to 7 years of transition will be.
